'''国忍富神'''（くにおしとみのかみ）は[[日本神話]]の[[神]]、または伝承上の人物。
== 概要 == [[古事記]]において[[大国主神]]の孫とされるが、神の性質は不明。
また、[[彦坐王]]の妃[[息長水依比売命]]は、[[天之御影命]]の7世孫「国忍富命」の娘とする。
大国主神の後裔を称する[[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]・[[須佐氏]]の系譜に、大国主神の御子神[[賀夜奈流美命]]の御子神に「国忍富命」が登場する。
== 系譜 == * 大国主神の説 ** [[鳥鳴海神]]系譜 :[[大国主神]]が[[八島牟遅能神]]の娘[[鳥取神]]を娶って生まれた[[鳥鳴海神]]が、[[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]]を娶って生んだ神。 :国忍富神は[[葦那陀迦神]]を娶り、[[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]]を生んでいる。
** [[賀夜奈流美命]]系譜 :大国主神の御子神[[賀夜奈流美命]]の御子神で、[[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]須佐氏の始祖。子は[[雲山命]]となっているが、妃神及び母神は不明。
* 天之御影命の説 [[天之御影命]]6世孫の[[坂戸彦命]]の子で5男1女をもうける。1女の[[息長水依比売命]]は[[彦坐王]]の妃となる。
== 祀る神社 == * [[富神社]]
== 関連項目 == * [[大国主神]] * [[鳥鳴海神]] * [[須佐神社]] * [[賀夜奈流美命]] * [[彦坐王]]
[[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]]

'''国忍富神'''（くにおしとみのかみ）は[[日本神話]]の[[神]]、または伝承上の人物。
== 概要 == [[古事記]]において[[大国主神]]の孫とされるが、神の性質は不明。
また、[[彦坐王]]の妃[[息長水依比売命]]は、[[天之御影命]]の7世孫「国忍富命」の娘とする。

** [[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]系譜 :大国主神の御子神[[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]の御子神で、[[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]須佐氏の始祖。子は[[雲山命]]となっているが、妃神及び母神は不明。

[[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]]

'''国忍富神'''（くにおしとみのかみ）は[[日本神話]]の[[神]]、または伝承上の人物。
== 概要 == [[古事記]]において[[大国主神]]の孫とされるが、神の性質は不明。十七世神（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱の[[国津神]]。
また、[[彦坐王]]の妃[[息長水依比売命]]は、[[天目一箇神|天之御影命]]の7世孫「国忍富命」の娘とする。

== 系譜 ==

'''国忍富神'''（くにおしとみのかみ）は[[日本神話]]の[[神]]、または伝承上の人物。
== 概要 == [[古事記]]において[[大国主神]]の孫とされるが、神の性質は不明。[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱の[[国津神]]。

'''国忍富神'''（くにおしとみのかみ）は[[日本神話]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]、または伝承上の人物。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』において[[大国主神]]の孫とされるが、名前のみ登場の神。『[[日本書紀]]』に記載はない。[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱の[[国津神]]。
「国」は「国土」、「忍」は「威圧的」、「富」は「豊富」を表し、名義は「国土が威圧的に豊富になること」と考えられ、母{{efn|日名照額田毘道男伊許地智邇神で名義は良田の神を親に持つ神霊}}の田の豊作を受け継いでいる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
また、[[彦坐王]]の妃[[息長水依比売命]]は、[[天目一箇神|天之御影命]]の7世孫「国忍富命」の娘とする。
大国主神の後裔を称する[[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]・[[須佐氏]]の系譜に、大国主神の御子神[[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]の御子神に「国忍富命」が登場する。

== 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] * 大国主神の説（神） ** [[鳥鳴海神]]系譜 **: [[大国主神]]が[[八島牟遅能神]]の娘[[鳥取神]]を娶って生まれた[[鳥鳴海神]]が、[[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]]を娶って生んだ神。 **: 国忍富神は[[葦那陀迦神]]を娶り、[[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]]を生んでいる。 ** ** [[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]系譜（神） **: 大国主神の御子神[[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]の御子神で、[[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]須佐氏の始祖。子は[[雲山命]]となっているが、妃神及び母神は不明。

'''国忍富神'''（くにおしとみのかみ）は[[日本神話]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]、または古代の人物。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』において[[大国主神]]の孫とされるが、名前のみ登場の神。『[[日本書紀]]』に記載はない。[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱の[[国津神]]。
「国」は「国土」、「忍」は「威圧的」、「富」は「豊富」を表し、名義は「国土が威圧的に豊富になること」と考えられ、母{{efn|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神で名義は良田の神を親に持つ神霊}}の田の豊作を受け継いでいる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
また、[[弥生時代]]の人物である国忍富命は[[天目一箇神|天之御影命]]の6世孫で、娘の[[息長水依比売命]]は『古事記』で[[彦坐王]]の妃とする<ref>[[中田憲信]]「東国諸国造」『諸系譜』第一冊。</ref>。
大国主神の後裔を称する[[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]・[[須佐氏]]の系譜に、大国主神の御子神[[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]の御子神に「国忍富命」が登場する。
== 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] * 大国主神の説（神） ** [[鳥鳴海神]]系譜 **: [[大国主神]]が[[八島牟遅能神]]の娘[[鳥取神]]を娶って生まれた[[鳥鳴海神]]が、[[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]]を娶って生んだ神。 **: 国忍富神は[[葦那陀迦神]]を娶り、[[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]]を生んでいる。 ** ** [[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]系譜（神） **: 大国主神の御子神[[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]の御子神で、[[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]須佐氏の始祖。子は[[雲山命]]となっているが、妃神及び母神は不明。
* 天之御影命の説（人物） *: [[天目一箇神|天之御影命]]6世孫の[[坂戸彦命]]の子で5男1女をもうける。[[三上氏]]の家系。1女の[[息長水依比売命]]は[[彦坐王]]の妃となる。

* 天之御影命の後裔（人物） *: [[天目一箇神|天之御影命]]6世孫の[[坂戸彦命]]の子で、妹に[[内色許男命]]の妻・[[新河小楯姫命]]と[[梨津臣命]]の妻である[[冨炊屋媛命]]がいる。また[[甲斐国造]]の祖である建勝日命を弟に挙げる説もある。 *: [[三上氏|三上祝]]、蒲生稲置、犬上県主、川上舎人、穴門国造（穴門直）、賀田直（荷田宿禰）の祖・[[大加賀美命]]、[[安国造]]、蒲生稲置、菅田首の祖・[[水穂真若命]]、鍛冶部、大湯坐部、桑名首、三村部、壬生宿禰、[[筑波国造]]、[[師長国造]]、[[須恵国造]]、[[馬来田国造]]、[[石背国造]]（磐瀬朝臣）、[[茨城国造]]、倭田中直、[[額田部氏|額田部連]]、[[菊多国造]]（湯坐菊多臣）、[[岐閉国造]]、[[周防国造]]（周防凡直）の祖・[[筑箪命]]、白根造の祖・[[加志岐弥命]]、[[彦坐王]]妃の[[息長水依比売命]]、磐隈媛命の4男2女がいる<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「第2章 天孫系氏族 第2節 三上氏族 1三上祝、三上直」『古代氏族系譜集成』中巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年、1066、1067頁。</ref>。

== 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] * 大国主神の後裔（神） ** [[鳥鳴海神]]系譜 **: [[大国主神]]が[[八島牟遅能神]]の娘[[鳥取神]]を娶って生まれた[[鳥鳴海神]]が、[[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]]を娶って生んだ神。 **: 国忍富神は[[葦那陀迦神]]を娶り、[[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]]を生んでいる。 ** ** [[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]系譜（神） **: 大国主神の御子神[[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]の御子神で、[[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]須佐氏の始祖。子は[[雲山命]]となっているが、妃神及び母神は不明。
* 天之御影命の後裔（人物） *: [[天目一箇神|天之御影命]]6世孫の[[坂戸彦命]]の子で、妹に[[内色許男命]]の妻・[[新河小楯姫命]]と[[梨津臣命]]の妻である[[冨炊屋媛命]]がいる。また[[甲斐国造]]の祖である建勝日命を弟に挙げる説もある。 *: [[三上氏|三上祝]]、蒲生稲置、犬上県主、川上舎人、穴門国造（穴門直）、賀田直（荷田宿禰）の祖・[[大加賀美命]]、[[安国造]]、蒲生稲置、菅田首の祖・[[水穂真若命]]、鍛冶部、大湯坐部、桑名首、三村部、壬生宿禰、[[筑波国造]]、[[師長国造]]、[[須恵国造]]、[[馬来田国造]]、[[石背国造]]（磐瀬朝臣）、[[茨城国造]]、倭田中直、[[額田部氏|額田部連]]、[[菊多国造]]（湯坐菊多臣）、[[岐閉国造]]、[[周防国造]]（周防凡直）の祖・[[筑箪命]]、白根造の祖・[[加志岐弥命]]、[[彦坐王]]妃の[[息長水依比売命]]、磐隈媛命の4男2女がいる<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「第2章 天孫系氏族 第2節 三上氏族 1三上祝、三上直」『古代氏族系譜集成』中巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年、1066、1067頁。</ref>。

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 国忍富神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第八代 | 先代= [[鳥鳴海神]] | 次代= [[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 国忍富神 | 別名 = 国忍富命 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->農業神 | 陵所 = | 父 = [[鳥鳴海神]] | 母 = [[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[葦那陀迦神]] | 子 = [[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = [[富神社]] }} {{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 国忍富命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[弥生時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = | 氏族 = [[三上氏|三上直]]祖 | 父母 = 父：[[坂戸毘古命]] | 兄弟 = [[新河小楯姫命]]、[[冨炊屋姫命]]、一説：[[建勝日命]] | 妻 = | 子 = [[大加賀美命]]、[[水穂真若命]]、[[筑箪命]]、[[加志岐弥命]]、[[息長水依比売命]]、[[磐隈媛命]] | 特記事項 = }} '''国忍富神'''（くにおしとみのかみ）は[[日本神話]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]、'''国忍富命'''（くにおしとみのみこと）は古代日本の人物。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』において[[大国主神]]の孫とされるが、名前のみ登場の神。『[[日本書紀]]』に記載はない。[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱の[[国津神]]。
「国」は「国土」、「忍」は「威圧的」、「富」は「豊富」を表し、名義は「国土が威圧的に豊富になること」と考えられ、母{{efn|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神で名義は良田の神を親に持つ神霊}}の田の豊作を受け継いでいる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
また、[[弥生時代]]の人物である国忍富命は[[天目一箇神|天之御影命]]の6世孫で、娘の[[息長水依比売命]]は『古事記』で[[彦坐王]]の妃とする<ref>[[中田憲信]]「東国諸国造」『諸系譜』第一冊。</ref>。

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 国忍富神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第八代 | 先代 = [[鳥鳴海神]] | 次代 = [[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 国忍富神 | 別名 = 国忍富命 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->農業神 | 陵所 = | 父 = [[鳥鳴海神]] | 母 = [[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[葦那陀迦神]] | 子 = [[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = [[富神社]] }}
'''国忍富神'''（くにおしとみのかみ）は[[日本神話]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』において[[大国主神]]の孫とされるが、名前のみ登場の神。『[[日本書紀]]』に記載はない。[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱の[[国津神]]。
「国」は「国土」、「忍」は「威圧的」、「富」は「豊富」を表し、名義は「国土が威圧的に豊富になること」と考えられ、母{{efn|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神で名義は良田の神を親に持つ神霊}}の田の豊作を受け継いでいる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
また、[[弥生時代]]の人物である国忍富命は[[天目一箇神|天之御影命]]の6世孫で、娘の[[息長水依比売命]]は『古事記』で[[彦坐王]]の妃とする<ref>[[中田憲信]]「東国諸国造」『諸系譜』第一冊。</ref>。
大国主神の後裔を称する[[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]・[[須佐氏]]の系譜に、大国主神の御子神[[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]の御子神に「国忍富命」が登場する。
== 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] ** [[鳥鳴海神]]系譜 **: [[大国主神]]が[[八島牟遅能神]]の娘の[[鳥取神]]を娶って生まれた[[鳥鳴海神]]が、[[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]]を娶って生んだ神。 **: 国忍富神は[[葦那陀迦神]]を娶り、[[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]]を生んでいる。 ** ** [[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]系譜（神） **: 大国主神の御子神[[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]の御子神で、[[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]須佐氏の始祖。子は[[雲山命]]となっているが、妃神及び母神は不明。
== 祀る神社 == * [[富神社]]（[[島根県]][[出雲市]][[斐川町]]）
== 参考文献 == {{Reflist}}
== 脚注 == {{Notelist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[大国主神]] * [[鳥鳴海神]] * [[須佐神社]] * [[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]] * [[彦坐王]] * [[天照大神]] * [[天津彦根命]] * [[天目一箇神|天之御影命]]
{{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くにおしとみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:三上氏]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:近江国の人物]] [[Category:滋賀県の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }} {{ill|{{PAGENAME}}|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.
== Family Tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:17 spirits of the world|ja|Category:十七世神|fr|Category:17 esprits du monde}} [[Category:Kunitsukami]] }}

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }} {{ill|{{PAGENAME}}|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.
== Family Tree == {{Eight generations of Izumo}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:17 spirits of the world|ja|Category:十七世神|fr|Category:17 esprits du monde}} [[Category:Kunitsukami]] }}

revision.comment: 外部リンクの追加など
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 国忍富神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第八代 | 先代 = [[鳥鳴海神]] | 次代 = [[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 国忍富神（クニオシトミノカミ） | 別名 = 国忍富命 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->農業神 | 陵所 = | 父 = [[鳥鳴海神]] | 母 = [[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[葦那陀迦神]] | 子 = [[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = 富神社 | 記紀等 = 古事記 }} '''国忍富神'''（国忍富命）とは、[[日本神話]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』において[[大国主神]]の孫とされるが、名前のみ登場の神。『[[日本書紀]]』に記載はない。[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱の国津神。
「国」は「国土」、「忍」は「威圧的」、「富」は「豊富」を表し、名義は「国土が威圧的に豊富になること」と考えられ、母{{efn|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神で名義は良田の神を親に持つ神霊}}の田の豊作を受け継いでいる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
大国主神の後裔を称する[[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]・須佐氏の系譜に、大国主神の御子神[[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]の御子神に「国忍富命」が登場する。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] ** [[鳥鳴海神]]系譜 **: [[大国主神]]が[[八島牟遅能神]]の娘の[[鳥取神]]を娶って生まれた[[鳥鳴海神]]が、[[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]]を娶って生んだ神。 **: 国忍富神は[[葦那陀迦神]]を娶り、[[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]]を生んでいる。 ** [[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]系譜（神） **: 大国主神の御子神[[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]の御子神で、[[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]須佐氏の始祖。子は[[雲山命]]となっているが、妃神及び母神は不明。 == 祀る神社 == * 富神社（[[島根県]][[出雲市]][[斐川町]]富村） == 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} == 脚注 == {{Notelist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大国主神]] * [[鳥鳴海神]] * [[須佐神社]] * [[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]] == 外部リンク == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/kunioshitominokami/ 国忍富神 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/9beb36014adc5c9140d85169aa10fe5b34462f88.html 富神社 - 神社詳細 | 島根県神社庁] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くにおしとみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:三上氏]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:近江国の人物]] [[Category:滋賀県の歴史]] [[Category:十七世神]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 国忍富神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第八代 | 先代 = [[鳥鳴海神]] | 次代 = [[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 国忍富神（クニオシトミノカミ） | 別名 = 国忍富命 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->農耕神 | 陵所 = | 父 = [[鳥鳴海神]] | 母 = [[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[葦那陀迦神]] | 子 = [[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]] | 宮 = | 神社 = 富神社 | 記紀等 = 古事記 }} '''国忍富神'''（国忍富命）とは、[[日本神話]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』において[[大国主神]]の孫とされるが、名前のみ登場の神。『[[日本書紀]]』に記載はない。[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱の国津神。
「国」は「国土」、「忍」は「威圧的」、「富」は「豊富」を表し、名義は「国土が威圧的に豊富になること」と考えられ、母{{efn|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神で名義は良田の神を親に持つ神霊}}の田の豊作を受け継いでいる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
大国主神の後裔を称する[[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]・須佐氏の系譜に、大国主神の御子神[[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]の御子神に「国忍富命」が登場する。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] ** [[鳥鳴海神]]系譜 **: [[大国主神]]が[[八島牟遅能神]]の娘の[[鳥取神]]を娶って生まれた[[鳥鳴海神]]が、[[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]]を娶って生んだ神。 **: 国忍富神は[[葦那陀迦神]]を娶り、[[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]]を生んでいる。 ** [[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]系譜（神） **: 大国主神の御子神[[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]の御子神で、[[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]須佐氏の始祖。子は[[雲山命]]となっているが、妃神及び母神は不明。 == 祀る神社 == * 富神社（[[島根県]][[出雲市]][[斐川町]]富村） == 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} == 脚注 == {{Notelist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大国主神]] * [[鳥鳴海神]] * [[須佐神社]] * [[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]] == 外部リンク == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/kunioshitominokami/ 国忍富神 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/9beb36014adc5c9140d85169aa10fe5b34462f88.html 富神社 - 神社詳細 | 島根県神社庁] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くにおしとみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:三上氏]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:近江国の人物]] [[Category:滋賀県の歴史]] [[Category:十七世神]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 国忍富神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第八代 | 先代 = [[鳥鳴海神]] | 次代 = [[ハヤミカノタケサハヤジヌミ|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 国忍富神（クニオシトミノカミ） | 別名 = 国忍富命 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->農耕神 | 陵所 = | 父 = [[鳥鳴海神]] | 母 = [[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[葦那陀迦神]] | 子 = 速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神 | 宮 = | 神社 = 富神社 | 記紀等 = 古事記 }} '''国忍富神'''（国忍富命）とは、[[日本神話]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』において[[大国主神]]の孫とされるが、名前のみ登場の神。『[[日本書紀]]』に記載はない。[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱の国津神。

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 国忍富神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第八代 | 先代 = [[鳥鳴海神]] | 次代 = [[ハヤミカノタケサハヤジヌミ|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 国忍富神（クニオシトミノカミ） | 別名 = 国忍富命 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->農耕神 | 陵所 = | 父 = 鳥鳴海神 | 母 = [[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[葦那陀迦神]] | 子 = 速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神 | 宮 = | 神社 = 富神社 | 記紀等 = [[古事記]] }} '''国忍富神'''（国忍富命）とは、[[日本神話]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『古事記』において[[大国主神]]の孫とされるが、名前のみ登場の神。『[[日本書紀]]』に記載はない。[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱の国津神。
「国」は「国土」、「忍」は「威圧的」、「富」は「豊富」を表し、名義は「国土が威圧的に豊富になること」と考えられ、母{{efn|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神で名義は良田の神を親に持つ神霊}}の田の豊作を受け継いでいる<ref>新潮日本古典集成 古事記</ref>。
大国主神の後裔を称する[[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]・須佐氏の系譜に、大国主神の御子神[[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]の御子神に「国忍富命」が登場する。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] * 鳥鳴海神系譜 ** [[大国主神]]が[[八島牟遅能神]]の娘の[[鳥取神]]を娶って生まれた鳥鳴海神が、日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神を娶って生んだ神。国忍富神は葦那陀迦神を娶り、速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神を生んでいる。 * 賀夜奈流美命系譜（神） ** 大国主神の御子神賀夜奈流美命の御子神で、須佐神社の社家須佐氏の始祖。子は雲山命となっているが、妃神及び母神は不明。

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 国忍富神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第八代 | 先代 = [[鳥鳴海神]] | 次代 = [[ハヤミカノタケサハヤジヌミ|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 国忍富神（クニオシトミノカミ） | 別名 = 國忍富神、国忍冨神、國忍冨神 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->農耕神 | 陵所 = | 父 = 鳥鳴海神 | 母 = [[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[葦那陀迦神]] | 子 = 速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神 | 宮 = | 神社 = 富神社 | 記紀等 = [[古事記]] }} '''国忍富神'''（クニオシトミ）とは、[[日本神話]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『古事記』において[[大国主神]]の孫とされるが、名前のみ登場の神。『[[日本書紀]]』に記載はない。[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱の国津神。

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 国忍富神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第八代 | 先代 = [[鳥鳴海神]] | 次代 = [[ハヤミカノタケサハヤジヌミ|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 国忍富神（クニオシトミノカミ） | 別名 = 國忍富神、国忍冨神、國忍冨神 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->[[農耕]]神 | 陵所 = | 父 = 鳥鳴海神 | 母 = [[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[葦那陀迦神]] | 子 = 速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神 | 宮 = | 神社 = 富神社 | 記紀等 = [[古事記]] }} '''国忍富神'''（クニオシトミ）とは、[[日本神話]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『古事記』において[[大国主神]]の孫とされるが、名前のみ登場の神。『[[日本書紀]]』に記載はない。[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱の国津神。

revision.comment: 2 revisions imported: importing my simple english wikipedia drafts for the 17 spirits of the world
revision.text:
{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }} {{ill|{{PAGENAME}}|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.
== Overview of the Generations == {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}} married {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|櫛名田比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime}} (daughter of [[Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi]]) and had {{ill|Yashimajinumi|ja|八島士奴美神|en}}<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yashimajinumi|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=181|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref>.
This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukafuchi no Mizuyarehana Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.

{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}} | type = Shinto | children = | father = | consort = | mother = }} {{ill|{{PAGENAME}}|ja}} is one of the {{ill|17 spirits of the world|simple|User:Immanuelle/17 spirits of the world|ja|十七世神|fr|User:Immanuelle/17 esprits du monde}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.

This god married {{Interlanguage link|Konohanachiruhime|ja|木花知流比売|en}}<ref name="auto"> (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi no Kami|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fuwanomochikunusunu Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.

This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.

The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}.

This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.

This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.

This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.

This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanotsutobetsuneru Kami}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funodomuno Kami|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funodomuno Kami}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (daughter of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神}}<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|last=Herbert|first=J.|publisher=Taylor & Francis|year=2010|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan|page=402|access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>.
This god married {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|fr}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana}}.
This god married {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine}} and had {{ill|Omizunu|ja|淤美豆奴神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Omizunu|en}}.
This god married {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|fr|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi}} (daughter of {{ill|Funozuno|ja|布怒豆怒神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Funozuno}}) and had {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref>. (This Amanofuyuorihime Kami is also known as Amanofukine Kami{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}, and is said to be the child of Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}. The child {{ill|Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto|ja|清武豊彦命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kiyotake Toyohiko no Mikoto}} is the ancestor of the {{ill|Ono Family (Shrine)|ja|小野氏 (社家)|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ono Family (Shrine)}} who were priests of {{ill|Hinomisaki Shrine|ja|日御崎神社社伝。}}{{Sfn|Kondo Toshitaka|1993}}.)
This god married {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Sashikuni Ōkami|ja|刺国大神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Ōkami|fr}}) and had {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Kami}} (Ōnamuchi no Kami. Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}. [[ja:国忍富神]] This god married {{ill|Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinaterunakatanomichioikochini Kami}} and had {{ill|Kunishirotomi Kami|ja|国忍富神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kunishirotomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Ikutamatakitamahime Kami|ja|活玉前玉比売神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ikutamatakitamahime Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami|ja|比比羅木之其花麻豆美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hihiraginosonohanamadomi Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mironami Kami|ja|美呂浪神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mironami Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Aonumamashinomaouhime|ja|青沼馬沼押比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Aonumamashinomaouhime}} (daughter of {{ill|Shikiyamanushi no Kami|ja|敷山主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shikiyamanushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami|ja|布忍富鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nunototomi Torinarumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Wakatsukime Kami|ja|若尽女神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Wakatsukime Kami}} and had {{ill|Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohibarudaisakanodomi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Totsumatone Kami|ja|遠津待根神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsumatone Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanosagiri Kami|ja|天之狭霧神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanosagiri Kami}}) and had {{ill|Totsuyamisakitara Kami|ja|遠津山岬多良斯神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Totsuyamisakitara Kami}}.

Half-sibling to the {{ill|Yaso Gods|ja|八十神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yaso Gods}}).
This god married {{ill|Tottori Kami|ja|鳥取神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tottori Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|Yashimamochinogami|ja|八島牟遅能神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Yashimamochinogami}}) and had {{ill|Torinarumi Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi Kami}}. This god married {{ill|Ashinataka Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka Kami}} and had {{ill|Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hayamike no Takekasayanosunumi Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Sakitamahime|ja|前玉比売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Sakitamahime}} (daughter of {{ill|Amanomikemunushi no Kami|ja|天之甕主神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanomikemunushi no Kami}}) and had {{ill|Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami|ja|甕主日子神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Mikemunushi no Hiko Kami}}.
This god married {{ill|Hinarashibime|ja|比那良志毘売|simple|User:Immanuelle/Hinarashibime}} (daughter of {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|淤加美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|en}}) and had {{ill|Tahirikishimarumi Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tahirikishimarumi Kami}}.

This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto}}.

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 国忍富神 | 世代名 = [[十七世神]] 第八代 | 先代 = [[鳥鳴海神]] | 次代 = [[ハヤミカノタケサハヤジヌミ|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[国津神]] | 全名 = 国忍富神（クニオシトミノカミ） | 別名 = 國忍富神、国忍冨神、國忍冨神 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの -->[[農耕]]神 | 陵所 = | 父 = 鳥鳴海神 | 母 = [[日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]] | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = [[葦那陀迦神]] | 子 = 速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神 | 宮 = | 神社 = 富神社 | 記紀等 = [[古事記]] }} '''国忍富神'''（クニオシトミ）とは、[[日本神話]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == 『古事記』において[[大国主神]]の孫とされるが、名前のみ登場の神。『[[日本書紀]]』に記載はない。[[十七世神]]（とおまりななよのかみ）の一柱の国津神。
「国」は「国土」、「忍」は「威圧的」、「富」は「豊富」を表し、名義は「国土が威圧的に豊富になること」と考えられ、母<ref group="注">日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神で、名義は良田の神を親に持つ神霊。</ref>の田の豊作を受け継いでいる{{Sfn|西宮|2014|p=382}}。
大国主神の後裔を称する[[須佐神社]]の[[社家]]・須佐氏の系譜に、大国主神の御子神[[カヤナルミ|賀夜奈流美命]]の御子神に「国忍富命」が登場する。 == 系譜 == [[画像:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|大国主の系図（『古事記』による）。青は男神、赤は女神、黄は性別不詳]] * 鳥鳴海神系譜{{Unbulleted list|[[大国主神]]が[[八島牟遅能神]]の娘の[[鳥取神]]を娶って生まれた鳥鳴海神が、日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神を娶って生んだ神。国忍富神は葦那陀迦神を娶り、速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神を生んでいる。}} * 賀夜奈流美命系譜（神）{{Unbulleted list|大国主神の御子神賀夜奈流美命の御子神で、須佐神社の社家須佐氏の始祖。子は雲山命となっているが、妃神及び母神は不明。}} == 祀る神社 == * 富神社（[[島根県]][[出雲市]][[斐川町]]富村） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === <references group="注" /> === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * <!-- にしみや -->{{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記 |volume= |date=2014年10月30日 |publisher=[[新潮社]] |ref={{SfnRef|西宮|2014}} |author=[[西宮一民]] 校注 |isbn=978-4-10-620801-0 |series=[[新潮日本古典集成]]（新装版）}} == 関連項目 == * [[布忍富鳥鳴海神]] == 外部リンク == * [https://kojiki.kokugakuin.ac.jp/shinmei/kunioshitominokami/ 国忍富神 – 國學院大學 古典文化学事業] * [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/9beb36014adc5c9140d85169aa10fe5b34462f88.html 富神社 - 神社詳細 | 島根県神社庁] {{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くにおしとみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:十七世神]] [[Category:農耕神]]

'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

== Shrines Dedicated ==
* '''Tomi Shrine''' ([[:en:Hikawa, Shimane|Hikawa Town]], [[:en:Izumo, Shimane|Izumo City]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]])

'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

The deity is one of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

== Shrines Dedicated ==
* '''Tomi Shrine''' ([[:en:Hikawa, Shimane|Hikawa Town]], [[:en:Izumo, Shimane|Izumo City]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]])
