'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the [[Category:17_spirits_of_the_world]]{{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

The deity is one of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

The deity is one of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

== Shrines Dedicated ==
* '''Tomi Shrine''' ([[:en:Hikawa, Shimane|Hikawa Town]], [[:en:Izumo, Shimane|Izumo City]], [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]])

'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}.

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}.

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

== Shrines Dedicated ==
* '''Tomi Shrine''' ({{ill|Hikawa, Shimane|en|Hikawa, Shimane|WD=Q1132034|lt=Hikawa Town}}, {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|WD=Q825166|lt=Izumo City}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}})

Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

== Shrines Dedicated ==
* '''Tomi Shrine''' ({{ill|Hikawa, Shimane|en|Hikawa, Shimane|WD=Q1132034|lt=Hikawa Town}}, {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|WD=Q825166|lt=Izumo City}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}})

'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}.

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the {{ill|The 17 Generations of Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The 17 Generations of Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

== Shrines Dedicated ==
* '''Tomi Shrine''' ({{ill|Hikawa, Shimane|en|Hikawa, Shimane|WD=Q1132034|lt=Hikawa Town}}, {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|WD=Q825166|lt=Izumo City}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}})

'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}.

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the {{ill|Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

'''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}.

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the {{ill|Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

== Shrines Dedicated ==
* '''Tomi Shrine''' ({{ill|Hikawa, Shimane|en|Hikawa, Shimane|WD=Q1132034|lt=Hikawa Town}}, {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|WD=Q825166|lt=Izumo City}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}})

The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. '''Kunioshitomi no Kami''' (国忍富神, クニオシトミ) is a deity in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}.

== Overview ==
Kunioshitomi no Kami is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'' as a grandchild of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, but no details about the deity are provided beyond the name. The deity is one of the {{ill|Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|qq=}}, classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

The name can be broken down into "Kuni" (land), "Oshi" (dominating or imposing), and "Tomi" (abundance), collectively interpreted as "the land is overwhelmingly abundant." The deity's role as an agricultural god is believed to reflect the prosperity inherited from {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}, who symbolizes fertile fields.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|p=382}}

The genealogy of the {{ill|Susa Shrine|ja|須佐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susa Shrine|qq=}}'s priestly Susa clan also mentions Kunioshitomi no Kami as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]

* '''Torinarumi-no-Kami Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|Kunioshitomi no Kami was born to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|ja|日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神|1=Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinateri-Nukada-Bichio-Ikochini-no-Kami|qq=}}. Kunioshitomi no Kami married {{ill|Ashinadaka no Kami|ja|葦那陀迦神|1=Ashinataka-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinadaka no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinataka-no-Kami|qq=}} and had a child, {{ill|Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|ja|速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|1=Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamikanotakesahayajinumi no Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamike-no-Takekasayanosunumi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.}}

* '''Kayanarumi Lineage'''  
{{Unbulleted list|In the genealogical records of Susa Shrine, Kunioshitomi no Kami is listed as a descendant of {{ill|Kayanarumi no Kami|ja|カヤナルミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi no Kami|qq=}}, an offspring of Ōkuninushi. The deity is also noted as the ancestor of the Susa clan, with a child named Kumoyama no Kami, though the consort and mother are not specified.}}

== Shrines Dedicated ==
* '''Tomi Shrine''' ({{ill|Hikawa, Shimane|en|Hikawa, Shimane|WD=Q1132034|lt=Hikawa Town|1=Hikawa, Shimane}}, {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|WD=Q825166|lt=Izumo City|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}})
